mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.0 percent indicates severe labor market slack and depressed consumer spending, which directly suppresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when unemployment remains above 8.0% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts reflect synchronized strength in consumer and residential investment demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values signal expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during late-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction or becomes noise (Rank IC < 0.01) when tested in early-cycle or recession regimes, or 20-day returns are negative in >50% of rising INDPRO observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index values exceeding 218.0 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of triggered instances, or the threshold effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth rate remains below 2.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, which supports corporate revenue expansion and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.02) when payrolls rise but unemployment stagnates, or 60-day returns are negative in >55% of aligned observations.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP levels reflect sustained aggregate demand growth, which directly improves corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03 over 3-year windows, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant correlation with GDPC1 levels (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index values falling below 98.0 indicate contracting manufacturing output and supply-side weakness, which suppresses earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold signal becomes statistically insignificant when tested against 5-day return distributions.
